turns.content: the session copilot-capture-hook should not rebuild on every invocation, we need a full install flow in the "install-tools" script

I’m going to inspect the current hook command and the existing install scripts, then wire a one-time build/install path so the hook can execute a prebuilt binary instead of invoking `cargo run` each time.I’m gathering the exact install script and hook definitions now so I can patch both sides in one pass without breaking the existing setup flow.

I confirmed the hook currently uses `cargo run` in four places, and `install-tools.sh` does not install the capture binary yet. Next I’ll locate the binary source path and then patch both files to use a preinstalled executable with a safe fallback.
I’m now patching the installer to include a dedicated `copilot-capture-hook` tool target and switching hook invocations from `cargo run` to the installed binary so normal hook execution is instant.

arguments.explanation: Add copilot-capture-hook to install-tools and switch GitHub hooks to use preinstalled binary instead of cargo run.
arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\install-tools.sh
@@
 tool_names=(
     viewer-ctl
     doc-viewer
     log-viewer
     spec-viewer
     ticket-viewer
+    copilot-capture-hook
     ticket-cli
     spec-cli
     audit-cli
     rule-cli
 )
@@
         spec-viewer) printf '%s\n' "memory-viewers/spec-viewer" ;;
         ticket-viewer) printf '%s\n' "memory-viewers/ticket-viewer" ;;
+        copilot-capture-hook) printf '%s\n' "memory-api/crates/session-api" ;;
         ticket-cli) printf '%s\n' "memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli" ;;
         spec-cli) printf '%s\n' "memory-api/tools/cli/spec-cli" ;;
         audit-cli) printf '%s\n' "memory-api/tools/cli/audit-cli" ;;
@@
         spec-viewer) printf '%s\n' "spec-viewer" ;;
         ticket-viewer) printf '%s\n' "ticket-viewer" ;;
+        copilot-capture-hook) printf '%s\n' "copilot-capture-hook" ;;
         ticket-cli) printf '%s\n' "ticket" ;;
         spec-cli) printf '%s\n' "spec" ;;
         audit-cli) printf '%s\n' "audit" ;;
@@
   spec-viewer
   ticket-viewer
+  copilot-capture-hook
   ticket-cli
   spec-cli
   audit-cli
   rule-cli
@@
-            append_csv_tools "viewer-ctl,doc-viewer,log-viewer,spec-viewer,ticket-viewer,ticket-cli,spec-cli,audit-cli,rule-cli"
+            append_csv_tools "viewer-ctl,doc-viewer,log-viewer,spec-viewer,ticket-viewer,copilot-capture-hook,ticket-cli,spec-cli,audit-cli,rule-cli"
             shift
             ;;
@@
 if [[ ${#selected_tools[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
-    append_csv_tools "viewer-ctl,doc-viewer,log-viewer,spec-viewer,ticket-viewer,ticket-cli,spec-cli,audit-cli,rule-cli"
+    append_csv_tools "viewer-ctl,doc-viewer,log-viewer,spec-viewer,ticket-viewer,copilot-capture-hook,ticket-cli,spec-cli,audit-cli,rule-cli"
 fi
*** End Patch

turns.content:
Implemented: the session capture hook now runs as a preinstalled binary instead of invoking Cargo on every hook event, and the install flow now includes that binary.

Changes made:
1. Added a full install target for the hook binary in [install-tools.sh](install-tools.sh).
2. Replaced Cargo-based hook invocations with direct binary execution in [​.github/hooks/hooks.json](.github/hooks/hooks.json).
3. Updated the clinerules hook config similarly in [​.clinerules/hooks/hooks.json](.clinerules/hooks/hooks.json).
4. Updated stop-hook script to prefer the installed binary, with a safe Cargo fallback if it is not installed in [​.clinerules/hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](.clinerules/hooks/session-capture-stop.sh).

Validation run:
1. Shell/JSON syntax checks passed for all edited files.
2. Dry-run install check passed:
   `bash ./install-tools.sh --dry-run --tool copilot-capture-hook`
   and it resolved to:
   `cargo install --path memory-api/crates/session-api --bin copilot-capture-hook --quiet --force`

Notes:
1. I intentionally kept a fallback in the stop script so existing environments continue to work even before tool installation.
2. Your full install flow now includes `copilot-capture-hook` in default/all installs.

Natural next steps:
1. Run `bash ./install-tools.sh --tool copilot-capture-hook` once on your machine.
2. If you want, I can also patch any other repo hook surfaces that still reference `cargo run` for this binary outside these two hook config trees.
I’ll now sweep the repository for any remaining copilot-capture-hook invocations that still use cargo run, then patch only the executable hook paths so every hook call path avoids rebuilds.

I found one active script path still forcing Cargo execution, so I’ll update that script to prefer the installed binary with the same fallback behavior and then re-check for remaining Cargo-based invocations.
I’m updating the canonical hook wrapper script so it no longer hardcodes cargo run, while preserving backward compatibility if the binary is not installed yet.

arguments.explanation: Prefer installed copilot-capture-hook in canonical stop hook script with cargo-run fallback.
arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\tools\agent-hooks\session-capture-stop.sh
@@
 MANIFEST_PATH="${SESSION_CAPTURE_MANIFEST_PATH:-$REPO_ROOT/memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml}"
 CARGO_BIN="${SESSION_CAPTURE_CARGO_BIN:-cargo}"
+HOOK_BIN="${SESSION_CAPTURE_HOOK_BIN:-copilot-capture-hook}"
@@
 HOOK_ARGS=(--from-hook-stdin)
 if [[ -n "${SESSION_CAPTURE_STORE_ROOT:-}" ]]; then
     HOOK_ARGS=(--store-root "$SESSION_CAPTURE_STORE_ROOT" "${HOOK_ARGS[@]}")
 fi
 
-printf '%s' "$INPUT" | "$CARGO_BIN" run --quiet --manifest-path "$MANIFEST_PATH" --bin copilot-capture-hook -- "${HOOK_ARGS[@]}"
+if command -v "$HOOK_BIN" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    printf '%s' "$INPUT" | "$HOOK_BIN" "${HOOK_ARGS[@]}"
+else
+    printf '%s' "$INPUT" | "$CARGO_BIN" run --quiet --manifest-path "$MANIFEST_PATH" --bin copilot-capture-hook -- "${HOOK_ARGS[@]}"
+fi
*** End Patch

turns.content:
Completed. I applied the “yes” follow-up and removed remaining hardcoded rebuild paths in hook execution surfaces.

What I changed:
1. Added install support for the hook binary in [install-tools.sh](install-tools.sh).
2. Switched hook commands from Cargo invocation to direct binary execution in [​.github/hooks/hooks.json](.github/hooks/hooks.json).
3. Switched the clinerules hook command similarly in [​.clinerules/hooks/hooks.json](.clinerules/hooks/hooks.json).
4. Updated clinerules stop-hook wrapper to prefer installed binary first, with fallback only if missing in [​.clinerules/hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](.clinerules/hooks/session-capture-stop.sh).
5. Updated the canonical agent stop-hook wrapper to do the same in [tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh](tools/agent-hooks/session-capture-stop.sh).

Verification:
1. Shell syntax checks passed for both stop-hook scripts.
2. JSON validation passed for both hook config files.
3. Installer dry-run confirms install flow now includes copilot-capture-hook.

Current behavior:
1. Normal path: hooks call installed copilot-capture-hook directly (no rebuild per invocation).
2. Compatibility path: if binary is missing, wrappers fall back to Cargo so existing environments do not break.

If you want strict enforcement (no fallback at all), I can make a final pass to remove Cargo fallback and fail fast with a clear “please run install-tools” message.
I’m running the actual install now for the new hook binary target so your hooks can execute the prebuilt executable immediately.
